old_claim.note: Protein Engineering is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: TALENs require engineered protein architecture: customized TALE repeat arrays provide DNA recognition and are fused to FokI nuclease domains for cleavage.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract describing TALENs as fusions of TAL effectors to FokI nuclease, with binding specificity determined by customizable polymorphic amino-acid repeat arrays.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper describes TALENs as TAL effector-FokI nuclease fusions whose DNA-binding specificity is determined by customizable amino-acid repeat arrays, and reports assembly of custom TALEN constructs.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a hard architecture edge because TALEN identity depends on engineered DNA-binding protein repeats fused to a nuclease domain, not merely on protein engineering as a background skill.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: TALENs remain modeled as a protein-engineered nuclease platform, now with primary-source evidence and a corrected first-known date.
would_reject_if: A source showed TALENs can exist without engineered TALE repeat arrays or protein-domain fusion architecture.
The TALEN node were broadened to all programmable genome-editing tools rather than transcription activator-like effector nucleases.
The correction removed sequencing or prior programmable-nuclease context needed for practical target selection and historical placement.
short_reason: TALENs are engineered protein-domain fusion nucleases, so protein engineering is a required architecture dependency.
